sections.paragraph: ἀϰατάπαστος, ον [ἀ-priv., ϰατάπαστος (κατά, πάσσω ‘sprinkle’) ‘besprinkled’ or (κατά, ἄπαστος ‘not eating’ fr. πατέομαι ‘eat’) ‘insatiable’] the sense ‘unbesprinkled’ according to the traditional etymology offers no sense for 2 Pt 2:14 v.l. If not a scribal error, the alternate derivation proposed by some textual critics in the sense insatiable fits the context.
